element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Core movement skill, reminiscent of Ninja Gaiden wall scaling
Basic projectile, powered by pellet cartridges

element_introduction_order.element: Minion encounters (Deadshot, KGBeast, Heat Wave, etc.)
element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Low-level DC Universe villains as recurring threats
Third projectile option, adds tactical variety
First skill test requiring learned mechanics
Confined spaces emphasizing wall jump skill
Penultimate challenge, skipped if Joker previously reached
Final stage, vertical emphasis with maximum difficulty
Ultimate test of all learned mechanics and weapons

typical_relief_triggers: Safe ground after wall jump sequences
typical_tension_triggers: Vertical climbing sequences requiring wall jump precision
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Frenetic gameplay alternating between platforming challenges and boss battles, with atmospheric cutscenes providing narrative breaks

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of wall jump mechanic and basic combat in Gotham Streets; noted as having a learning curve but balanced difficulty
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Industrial complexity increases in Axis Chemical Plant and Sewers; machine-based bosses escalate challenge; weapon switching becomes more tactical
difficulty_curve.late_game: Laboratory and Cathedral stages introduce maximum enemy density and environmental complexity; culminates in Joker final boss with all mechanics required
notable_spikes: Killer Moth boss requiring mastery of wall jump timing
Machine Intelligence System introducing mechanical threats
Joker final boss combining all previous challenges

notable_level_patterns.description: Movement from urban streets through chemical plants to sewers, escalating environmental hazards and mechanical threats
Wall jump mechanic introduced in streets, refined in sewers with confined spaces, mastered in cathedral belltower
Each level culminates in distinct boss encounter; penultimate Firebug skipped on replay to allow direct Joker access
Three projectile weapons (batarang, batdisk, batpoon) required for different combat situations; cartridge-based ammo system adds resource management
